title: Potato Malai Kofta
subtitle: with Sautéed Spinach & Tomato Chutney

steps.text: Peel and dice the potato into bite-size pieces. Add the diced potato to a medium saucepan and cover with 1 inch salted water. Bring to a boil and cook until fork-tender, 10 to 12 minutes.

Peel and chop the onion(s). Peel and slice the garlic. Peel and mince 2 tsp (4 tsp) ginger. Peel and grate the carrot(s) on the largest side of a box grater. Once the potatoes are fork-tender, add the grated carrot and green peas to the saucepan with the potatoes and cook for 1 minute. Drain potato and carrot mixture and transfer to a large bowl.

Return the empty saucepan to medium heat with 2 tsp (4 tsp) olive oil. Add the chopped onion, sliced garlic, and minced ginger. Cook until the vegetables are softened, 3 to 5 minutes. Add the tomato powder, curry powder, coconut milk, and ½ tsp (1 tsp) salt, and bring sauce to a simmer. Cook until malai sauce is slightly thickened, 2 to 3 minutes.

Add the garbanzo bean flour and ¼ tsp (½ tsp) salt to the large bowl with the potato and carrot mixture, and lightly mash until there are only a few lumps. Form the potato kofta mixture into 6 (12) patties about 1 inch thick.
Heat a large nonstick skillet over medium-high heat with 2 tbsp olive oil. Add the potato kofta and cook until browned and crisp, 1 to 2 minutes per side. Transfer the seared potato kofta to a plate.

Return the skillet to medium heat with 1 tsp (2 tsp) olive oil. Once hot, add the spinach and a sprinkle of salt and pepper. Cook until wilted, 2 to 3 minutes. Transfer the malai sauce to a blender and blend on low until smooth. Divide the malai sauce between plates and top with sautéed spinach and potato kofta. Serve with tomato chutney for dipping. Enjoy!

tips: TIP: Don't drain the potatoes yet because we'll add vegetables in step 2.
TIP: If you're making the 4 serving recipe, cook the kofta in batches.
TIP: Vent blender (by removing the plastic center of lid) to avoid splashing.
Substitute: another green for baby spinach
